tweet: So while we certainly still have much work to do, we continue to step forward. Sometimes imperceptibly so, sometimes in a leap, but forward nonetheless. #ThankYouMLK50

name: Tim Scott
tweet: When nine parishioners were killed at Mother Emanuel in 2015, the killer sought a race war. Perhaps at some point in our nation’s history, his plan would have worked. But not now. We are stronger from your teachings, more unified by your passion, & fortified by your words.

name: Tim Scott
tweet: There are obvious ways, such as the end of Jim Crow laws and the Civil Rights Act, but also ways we perhaps do not think about…ways your legacy has endured, even through tragedy. #ThankYouMLK50

name: Tim Scott
tweet: But even though you were stolen from this world too young, your legacy endures. The change we have seen since you and my grandfather were born in the 1920s, not just in the South but across the country, has been massive. #ThankYouMLK50

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 20. I’m also not going to stop looking for a solution – giving up isn’t what we were sent here to do.

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 19. I’m not going to give up on repealing the Unaffordable Care Act. American families deserve better.

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 18. I truly believe we all want what’s best for our nation and her people. To me, that’s finding a better solution than the ACA provides.

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 17. But here’s what I’m not doing – I’m not going to impugn the intentions of people who disagree

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 16. You can’t just put your head in the sand and ignore those facts and hope that it lives forever

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 15. Its price tag grew and grew from the billions into the trillions, and finally scoring agencies just gave up

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 14. The ACA is not sustainable, and hasn’t been from the beginning. Its foundation was built on certain groups that never signed up

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 13. I remain committed to repealing the ACA and replacing it with a system that works for all families

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 12. So that’s where we stand today. A bad program that has grown deep roots, and a bitterly divided political culture.

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 11. We’ve got to realize that our pattern should not be disagree and destroy, but rather disagree and solve

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 10. And two – along the way we stopped fighting the policy, and started fighting the people behind it

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 9. No matter how badly the program serves (or doesn’t serve) the people – replacing an entitlement is hard

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 8. The ACA went into effect, and – “A government bureau is the nearest thing to eternal life we’ll ever see on this earth” (Reagan)

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 7. Two things happened along the way to where we stand today

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 6. These are facts – you can’t argue with them. And that’s not how our health care system should work

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 5. Not everyone got to keep their doctors, and they weren’t able to keep their plans

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 4. Millions are paying for insurance they don’t want. In SC, more than 100,000 stuck without an option

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 3. In South Carolina, we’re down to one insurer offering plans on the exchanges.We’re seeing triple digit premium hikes,sky high deductibles

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 2. The debt continues to grow, and the ACA is not getting any better. The former isn’t helped by the latter.

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 1.When I was elected to Congress in 2010–along w/a record-breaking Freshman class,the major platform was repealing the ACA&the national debt

name: Tim Scott
tweet: Because without education, incarceration rates go up, unemployment goes up, lifetime earnings go down.

name: Tim Scott

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 16. God Bless, and I hope you all have a great day. Remember - if you want to walk on water, you have got to get out of the boat.

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 15. Your passion, dedication and love are the heartbeat of this nation - and underscore what could happen when we stand united.

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 14. For my mom, my granddad, and everyone else who sacrificed so much so the next generation could live a better life - I say thank you.

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 13. I am working every day to bring quality education, good jobs & the American Dream back to those who may not have experienced them yet

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 12. That vision must continue to guide us into the future. To truly help us all, we must offer a hand to those in need.

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 11. Our great nation was built on the dreams and ideals of a group of people who recognized that all men are created equal

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 10. We can come together, and invest in our communities, encourage private dollars to help rebuild and restore.

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 9. Let’s recognize the manufacturing boom in states like SC, and reemphasize technical training, apprenticeships, and good, well paying jobs

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 8. Every child must have access to a quality education. Unemployment, incarceration, dropout rates - all linked to educational achievement

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 7. To turn back on the light at the end of the tunnel, and strengthen our nation from the ground up.

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 6. And so, its up to us to bring it back to those millions of families across America who are desperately seeking a chance at success

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 5. When you can’t see beyond your own block, when you don’t know what’s possible, the American Dream starts to fade.

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 4. I grew up in a place like that, and have seen both the short- and long-term effects this can bring.

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 3. While we are the greatest nation on earth, we still have too many people living in poverty and in communities where hope has evaporated

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 2. We must stand together, move forward together, and find solutions together.

name: Tim Scott
tweet: 1. I stand for opportunity. For the belief that every single person in this country has greatness inside them waiting to be unleashed.
